mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, discount rates and recession fear both ease, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with higher subsequent equity returns.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of months where FEDFUNDS declines versus the prior 3 months, or if the conditional average return is not above the unconditional mean in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation rate that falls below recent trend, while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation trend, signals improving real-rate pressure and supports equity multiples over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails when inflation is reaccelerating and the rate path is already restrictive.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change falls for 2 consecutive monthly prints
failure_condition: Rejected if CPI disinflation triggers are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal loses sign consistency when FEDFUNDS is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: An upward break in UNRATE above its recent level signals labor-market deterioration and rising recession probability, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism is strongest when PAYEMS growth is also softening.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if labor-deterioration triggers are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average return after the trigger is not materially below the unconditional 5-day mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO after a prior slowdown indicates improving industrial supply and earnings throughput, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is falsified if output acceleration does not translate into positive forward equity performance.
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month INDPRO acceleration than after flat or declining INDPRO, or if the rank correlation stays near zero in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST is rising, household and retail demand is broadening, which supports corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction fails if retail sales strength is not accompanied by housing activity.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth turns positive and HOUST is above its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint RSAFS-HOUST trigger does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if either proxy alone explains the same effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in GDPC1 indicates weakening aggregate demand and lower expected earnings, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is falsified if GDP slowdowns are followed by continued equity gains.
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in a majority of quarters where GDPC1 growth decelerates, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean over rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, the labor market is clearly deteriorating and equities should weaken over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is specific to simultaneous payroll losses and unemployment gains, not either series alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises at least 0.2 percentage points over the same span
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint deterioration trigger is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value beyond PAYEMS or UNRATE separately.

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive policy regime, CPIAUCSL easing combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.5 percent marks a likely peak in real policy tightness, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect fails outside high-rate tightening regimes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5 and CPIAUCSL monthly inflation decelerates versus the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if the trigger does not precede positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average post-trigger return is not above the return distribution for non-tightening periods.
